questions.question: Barak-8 missile was jointly developed by India and ______?
questions.explanation: Long-range surface to air missile (LRSAM) jointly developed by Israel and India. LRSAM, or Barak-8 naval air defense system, is jointly developed by India's Defense Research and Development Organization (DRDO) in close collaboration with Israel's Israel Aircraft Industry.

questions.question: Which among the following is an air-to-air missile?
questions.explanation: The Astra is an active radar homing beyond-visual-range air-to-air missile (BVRAAM) developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation.

questions.question: College of Defence Management is in which state?
questions.explanation: College of Defence Management (CDM) is a Ministry of Defence sponsored institute of management aimed at providing scientific training to selected armed forces officers. It is in Secunderabad, Telangana.

questions.question: Who was the First defence minister of India?
questions.explanation: The first defence minister of independent India was Baldev Singh, who served in Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru's cabinet during 1947 - 52.

questions.question: Prabal Dostyk is a joint military exercise between India and _________?
questions.explanation: 'Prabal Dostyk' meaning 'Robust Friendship' will be a significant step towards fostering military as well as diplomatic ties between troops of Indian & Kazakhstan Army.

questions.question: Which among the following Indian aircraft was developed under Light Combat Aircraft programme?
questions.explanation: The HAL Tejas is an Indian single-seat, single-jet engine, multirole light fighter. It came from the Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) programme, which began in the 1980s to replace India's ageing MiG-21 fighters.

questions.question: Which Indian Naval Ship is a modified version of Admiral Gorshkov aircraft carrier of Russia?
questions.explanation: INS Vikramaditya is a modified Kiev-class aircraft carrier. The carrier served with the Soviet Navy and later with the Russian Navy as Admiral Gorshkov.

questions.question: Protection and Security is the motto of which among the following defence forces?
questions.explanation: It is the motto of CISF. The Central Industrial Security Force is a Central Armed Police Force in India. It was set up under an Act of the Parliament of India on 10 March 1969.

questions.question: Which among the following military operations is related to Kargil war?
questions.explanation: The Kargil War was an armed conflict between India and Pakistan that took place between May and July 1999. The conflict is also referred to as Operation Vijay.

questions.question: Which is a wartime gallantary award?
questions.explanation: Vir Chakra is an Indian gallantry award presented for acts of bravery in the battlefield.

questions.question: Which among the following forms the base in hierarchical arrangement of taxonomical classification?
Who among the following devised five kingdom classification?
questions.explanation: R.H. Whittaker (1969) proposed a Five Kingdom Classification. The kingdoms defined by him were named Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae and Animalia.

questions.question: Which organism is a part of Kingdom Monera?
questions.explanation: Bacteria are the sole members of the Kingdom Monera. They are the most abundant micro-organisms.

questions.question: Organisms that derive nutrition from dead substances is referred to as ___________?
questions.explanation: A saprophyte or saprotroph is an organism which gets its energy from dead and decaying organic matter. Example- Fungi.

questions.question: Organism that derive nutrients from a living organism, depriving the host the nutrition is referred to as ____________?
questions.explanation: Parasites are organisms that lives on or in an organism of another species, known as the host, from the body of which it obtains nutriment.

questions.question: Which among the following about viruses is TRUE?
options: They contain both RNA and DNA
They contain either RNA or DNA
questions.explanation: In addition to proteins viruses also contain genetic material, that could be either RNA or DNA. No virus contains both RNA and DNA. A virus is a nucleoprotein and the genetic material is infectious.

questions.question: Lichens are symbiotic associations of algae and _________?
questions.explanation: Lichens are symbiotic associations i.e. mutually useful associations, between algae and fungi. The algal component is known as phycobiont and fungal component as mycobiont, which are autotrophic and heterotrophic, respectively.

questions.question: Which among the following product of algae is used in preparations of ice cream?
questions.explanation: Agar, one of the commercial products obtained from Gelidium and Gracilaria are used to grow microbes and in preparations of ice-creams and jellies.

questions.question: Which among the part of a flower develops into a fruit?
questions.explanation: Ovules develop into seeds and the ovaries develop into fruit. Macrosporangium is also called as ovule.

questions.question: Which organelle in an animal cell is non-membrane bound organelle?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are non-membrane bound organelles found in all cells - both eukaryotic as well as prokaryotic. Within the cell, ribosomes are found in the cytoplasm, chloroplasts (in plants) and mitochondria and on rough ER.

questions.question: Which among the following cell organelle helps in cell division?
questions.explanation: Animal cells contain a non-membrane bound organelle called centriole which helps in cell division.

questions.question: Chemical reactions that keep cells alive takes place in which among the following region?
questions.explanation: The cytoplasm is the main arena of cellular activities in both the plant and animal cells. Various chemical reactions occur in it to keep the cell in the 'living state'.

questions.question: Osmosis in a cell is a process of _______?
questions.explanation: Water move across cell membrane from higher to lower concentration. Movement of water by diffusion is called osmosis.

questions.question: Which among the following teeth helps a person to pronounce words?
questions.explanation: At the front of the mouth are eight thin, straight teeth called incisors - four at the top and four at the bottom - which bite into the food eaten and help in pronouncing words. Incisors also support the lips.

questions.question: There are totally how many types of RNA?
questions.explanation: There are three types of RNA - mRNA, rRNA and tRNA which actually carry out the protein synthesis in the cell based on the coded message for proteins provided by DNA.

questions.question: Which among the following vitamins is NOT fat soluble?
questions.explanation: Vitamins which are soluble in water but insoluble in fat and oils are called water soluble vitamins. B and C come under this group. The vitamins A, D, E and K are fat soluble. They are stored in liver and adipose (fat storing) tissues.

questions.question: Bleeding of gums is a symptom for deficiency of which vitamin?
questions.explanation: Symptoms of scurvy result from severe vitamin C deficiency. They include bleeding sores, gums, tooth loss, anemia, and a reduced rate of healing for injuries.

questions.question: Which among the following genetic disorder is referred to as trisomy 21?
questions.explanation: Down syndrome (DS or DNS), also known as trisomy 21, is a genetic disorder caused by the presence of all or part of a third copy of chromosome 21.

questions.question: Which is referred to as laughing gas?
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide is commonly known as laughing gas. It is due to the euphoric effects upon inhaling it.

questions.question: Which is NOT a constituent of the alloy Cunife?
questions.explanation: Cunife is an alloy of copper (Cu), nickel (Ni), iron (Fe), and in some cases cobalt (Co).
